title: Great Ashfield Airdrome — 385th Bomb Group Memorial
location_description: Great Ashfield village / Stowmarket Suffolk near A1088
rationale_for: 1) Community advocate (Mary Blanche) has submitted photos and a PDF — active stakeholder ready to engage. 2) Physical memorial already exists — interpretation panel on riverside and memorial plaque confirmed. 3) Site is in Suffolk which has several of our entries already. 4) We already cover USAAF entries (Kessingland B-24 415th BG and others). 5) 385th BG is well-documented — americanairmuseum.com and 385thbombgroup.com have rich records. 6) 296 combat missions and 129 aircraft lost — significant scale of sacrifice. 7) Fits the BMR mission: British-based memorial to aircrews who gave their lives.
rationale_against: 1) This is an airfield memorial rather than a specific crash site — most BMR entries mark a specific aircraft loss. 2) Could set a precedent for including any wartime airfield without a specific crash narrative. 3) Scope question: do we cover whole-unit memorials or single-incident crash sites? 4) 385th BG flew from Great Ashfield but the bulk of losses were over occupied Europe not in the UK.
recommended_decision: ADD — with scoped narrative focusing on specific aircraft losses linked to the memorial
missing_data_needed: GPS coordinates for both memorial locations (interpretation panel + plaque); specific crew losses commemorated on the plaque; parish council contact; any trust or association maintaining the memorial; warmemorialsonline.org.uk entry; whether 385thbombgroup.com association is still active
